claim: T regulatory cells (tTregs) lacking αvβ8 are more adept at suppressing pathogenic T-cell responses during active inflammation.

result.claim: T regulatory cells (tTregs) lacking αvβ8 are more adept at suppressing pathogenic T-cell responses during active inflammation.
result.reasoning: The claim is directly refuted by evidence from paper ID 17997584, titled "Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation". This paper states that "Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation" (Proposition ID: prop_2760). The abstract of the paper further confirms this finding, indicating that the αvβ8 integrin is essential for Treg cells to suppress T-cell-mediated inflammation during active inflammation. This directly contradicts the claim that tTregs lacking αvβ8 are *more* adept at suppressing these responses.

evidence.text: TGF-β-producing CD4(+) T cells play a protective role in F11r(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Compromised intestinal epithelial barrier induces adaptive immune compensation that protects from colitis.

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype correlates with diminished IL-2 responsiveness in Ag-experienced CD4(+) T cells, as measured by phosphorylation of STAT5a.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Golli-deficient T cells showed enhanced calcium entry upon T cell receptor stimulation.
evidence.source: Golli protein negatively regulates store depletion-induced calcium influx in T cells.

evidence.text: Mouse studies indicate that tissue localization of T cell subsets is important for both protective immunity and immunoregulation.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: The presence of an autoimmune disease-associated IL2RA haplotype is associated with a reduction in the ability of Tregs to suppress proliferation of autologous effector T cells.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Th1-type cytokine production was decreased in the gp130FXXQ/FXXQ immune system.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: T-zone chemokines guide activated B cells to the T zone.
evidence.source: Finding the right niche: B-cell migration in the early phases of T-dependent antibody responses.

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells comprise 30-40% of CD4(+) T cells in pediatric tissues.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: In adult tissues the main subset of T cells are memory T cells.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: Autospecific CD4(+) T cells proliferated poorly in the absence of IL-12.
evidence.source: Dual role of the IL-12/IFN-gamma axis in the development of autoimmune myocarditis: induction by IL-12 and protection by IFN-gamma.

evidence.text: Treg-cell-specific deletion of integrin αvβ8 did not result in a spontaneous inflammatory phenotype.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: T cell activation is predicated on the interaction between the T cell receptor and peptide-major histocompatibility ligands.
evidence.source: The stimulatory potency of T cell antigens is influenced by the formation of the immunological synapse.

evidence.text: Treg cells lacking expression of integrin αvβ8 were unable to suppress pathogenic T cell responses during active inflammation.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: Antigen-specific CD8+ T-cell tolerance is induced by myeloid-derived suppressor cells (MDSCs).
evidence.source: Altered recognition of antigen is a mechanism of CD8+ T cell tolerance in cancer

evidence.text: Interleukin-17A (IL-17A)-producing IL-7Rα(+) innate lymphoid cells (ILCs) were potent promoters of disease in Tbx21(-/-)Rag2(-/-) ulcerative colitis (TRUC) mice.
evidence.source: The Transcription Factor T-bet Regulates Intestinal Inflammation Mediated by Interleukin-7 Receptor+ Innate Lymphoid Cells

evidence.text: Reduced IL-2 production correlates with reduced function of CD4+ CD25+ regulatory T cells.
evidence.source: Interleukin-2 gene variation impairs regulatory T cell function and causes autoimmunity

evidence.text: Regulatory T (T(reg)) cells are present at much lower frequencies (1-10%) in adult tissues.
evidence.source: Early life compartmentalization of human T cell differentiation and regulatory function in mucosal and lymphoid tissues

evidence.text: CD4(+)CD25(+) regulatory T cells (Tregs) from individuals with a range of human autoimmune diseases are deficient in their ability to control autologous proinflammatory responses when compared with nondiseased, control individuals.
evidence.source: Type 1 diabetes-associated IL2RA variation lowers IL-2 signaling and contributes to diminished CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cell function.

evidence.text: Effector Treg cells express high amounts of the integrin αvβ8.
evidence.source: Integrin αvβ8-Mediated TGF-β Activation by Effector Regulatory T Cells Is Essential for Suppression of T-Cell-Mediated Inflammation

evidence.text: Mice lacking the transcription factor T-bet in the innate immune system develop microbiota-dependent colitis.
evidence.source: The Transcription Factor T-bet Regulates Intestinal Inflammation Mediated by Interleukin-7 Receptor+ Innate Lymphoid Cells
